name: Ph.D. in Environmental Science

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The 37-course inventory gives a research doctorate broad, coherent interdisciplinary reach: environmental chemistry and analysis (EVSC 610/612/711, CHEM 764), engineering treatment and water chemistry (ENE 663/664/665/672, ENE 630), remediation and hazardous-substance management (ENE 662, EVSC 622/712), toxicology and risk (EVSC 614/616), microbiology (EVSC 627, ENE 661), and policy/economics (EPS 612/614/622/638), anchored by EVSC 792B Pre-Doctoral Research. Coursework appropriately supports dissertation work, though requirementGroups is 0 so any required breadth structure is not displayed.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the doctoral plan of study clarifying required breadth areas across chemistry, engineering, and policy, plus the qualifier-linked coursework and research-credit progression.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: deepestPrereqChain is 1 with sensible links (ENE 664 on ENE 663 Water Chemistry, ENE 662 on EM 631, EVSC 711 on EVSC 612), but missingCourseCount is 1: EVSC 790A is referenced yet absent, a dangling doctoral research/dissertation code that should resolve.
dimensions.suggestion: Fix the dangling EVSC 790A reference by adding or correcting the doctoral research/dissertation record so the research-credit chain is complete.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or doctoral competencies are present in the data, leaving the independent-research and breadth expectations of the Ph.D. unstated.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 3-5 doctoral learning outcomes covering original environmental research, interdisciplinary breadth, and scholarly communication.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is null with no parsed plan, so the coursework-versus-dissertation credit structure is not transparent; for a research doctorate the total is less central than for a master's, and the null creditReconcileDelta is a graduate-appropriate transparency note rather than an internal inconsistency.
dimensions.suggestion: State the total credit requirement and the split between required/elective coursework and dissertation-research credits.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: Most descriptions are present and specific (e.g., ENE 664 Physical and Chemical Treatment, EVSC 614 Quantitative Risk Assessment), but several referenced courses carry empty descriptions (EVSC 622 Bioremediation, ENE 630 Physical Processes of Environmental Systems, ENE 661 Environmental Microbiology) and the EVSC 790A dangling reference is a visible completeness defect.
dimensions.suggestion: Fill the empty course descriptions (EVSC 622, ENE 630, ENE 661), resolve EVSC 790A, and add a doctoral plan-of-study section.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The elective breadth is current and applied, covering site remediation, bioremediation, hazardous-substance management, sustainability and life-cycle analysis (ENE 673), energy and sustainability (EVSC 715, CHE 724), and quantitative risk, aligning the doctorate with environmental research, consulting, and regulatory frontiers; the main gap is quantitative climate and environmental-data-science content.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a climate-science/modeling course and an environmental-data-science/geospatial-analytics elective to modernize the quantitative research toolkit.

descriptionQuality.justification: Where present, descriptions are specific and research-relevant (EVSC 711 advanced environmental analysis, EVSC 712 hazardous-substance management), but several referenced courses (EVSC 622, ENE 630, ENE 661) have empty description fields, weakening overall completeness.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisite links that exist are clear and sensibly gated (ENE 663 to 664, EVSC 612 to 711), but the single dangling reference (EVSC 790A, missingCourseCount 1) and a cross-listed prerequisite style slightly reduce internal consistency.

missingTopics.rationale: EVSC 615 addresses global problems descriptively, but no course delivers quantitative climate or earth-system modeling expected of a doctoral program today.

missingTopics.topic: Environmental data science and geospatial ML
missingTopics.rationale: The program lacks a computational/geospatial-analytics course; doctoral environmental research increasingly depends on remote sensing, spatial statistics, and machine learning.
The toxicology, analysis, and treatment courses predate the PFAS/microplastics research agenda now central to the field and to funding calls.
The list is chemistry- and engineering-weighted with little ecological-systems or biodiversity research content for a comprehensive environmental doctorate.
Water, soil, and waste are well covered, but atmospheric chemistry and air-quality modeling are largely absent from the elective set.

emergingTrends.trend: Climate resilience, adaptation, and decarbonization research
emergingTrends.rationale: Funding and policy priorities are shifting toward adaptation and net-zero pathways that EPS 622, ENE 673, and EVSC 715 could anchor into a research strand.

emergingTrends.trend: PFAS and emerging-contaminant fate and remediation
emergingTrends.rationale: PFAS chemistry and treatment is a rapidly funded frontier that EVSC 622 bioremediation and the ENE treatment courses could be extended to address.

emergingTrends.trend: Environmental sensing networks and machine learning
emergingTrends.rationale: Sensor-plus-ML monitoring is transforming environmental measurement, a data-driven direction the analytical strand (EVSC 711) should incorporate.
ENE 673's life-cycle framing positions the program to formalize materials-recovery and circular-economy research.
Distributional and equity dimensions are increasingly central to environmental scholarship, extending the EPS policy cluster.

topSuggestions: Resolve the dangling EVSC 790A reference and fill the empty course descriptions (EVSC 622, ENE 630, ENE 661) to complete the entry.
Publish the doctoral plan of study (required breadth, qualifier-linked coursework, dissertation-credit progression) and state total credits.
Add climate-science/modeling and environmental-data-science/geospatial-ML electives to modernize the quantitative toolkit.
Introduce emerging-contaminant (PFAS/microplastics) research content across the toxicology and treatment strands.
Publish 3-5 doctoral learning outcomes describing original-research, interdisciplinary-breadth, and communication competencies.
